q: Which statement correctly describes the lymphatic system's role in fluid balance?
options: Lymphatic vessels return excess tissue fluid directly to the liver for detoxification
Lymph capillaries actively pump fluid back into the venous system using muscular contractions
Excess tissue fluid not reabsorbed at the venous end of capillaries drains into lymph ducts and is returned to blood
The lymphatic system produces plasma proteins to maintain osmotic pressure in capillaries
explanation: Not all tissue fluid is reabsorbed at the venous end of capillaries; the excess enters blind-ended lymph capillaries and is eventually returned to the bloodstream at the subclavian veins.
